user_input: How many letters of recommendation do I need for the MS in Applied Data Science?
retrieved_contexts: ['The MS in Applied Data Science program requires two letters of recommendation. We strongly recommend that at least one letter is written by someone such as a direct manager/supervisor or internship supervisor who can attest to skills you demonstrated or gained though a professional workplace experience (e.g., leadership, teamwork, collaboration, initiative, management, other).', 'Note: Letters of recommendation can be uploaded after you submit your application. Recommended sources for letters of recommendation include: professors; supervisors or managers; professional mentors.']
response: The MS in Applied Data Science program requires two letters of recommendation [1]. One of these letters is strongly recommended to come from a supervisor or manager who can attest to your professional skills and experiences [1]. Letters can be uploaded after application submission and are typically provided by professors, supervisors, or professional mentors [2].
reference: The program requires two letters of recommendation. They strongly recommend that at least one come from a direct manager, supervisor, or internship supervisor who can speak to your professional skills. Letters from family members, friends, or peers are not accepted.

user_input: Is the GRE or GMAT required to apply?
retrieved_contexts: ['No, the GRE/GMAT is not required for admissions.', 'While the GRE/GMAT is not required, applicants can still submit their scores. The GRE school code is 1832; the GMAT school code is H9X-WG-70.', 'As part of the online application, candidates will be required to submit a GMAT or GRE score for the joint program. International applicants may be required to submit proof of English language proficiency by submitting a TOEFL iBT or IELTS test score. The minimum TOEFL iBT score required for admission is 104; the minimum IELTS score required is 7. Proof of English proficiency may be waived under certain criteria noted by UChicago GRAD Admissions.', 'When applying to the program, only your unofficial transcripts are required. If you are admitted into the program, then official transcripts must be sent to the following (note a bachelor’s degree is required to apply to the program):', '- Undergraduate or graduate GPA', 'Only completed applications are sent for committee review.', 'One video prompt response is required; the other is optional. Each video should be approximately 1:30 minutes in length.', 'Applicants to the Master’s in Applied Data Science program who do not meet the English Language Proficiency criteria must submit proof of English language proficiency.']
response: The GRE or GMAT is not required for the Master’s in Applied Data Science program [1]. However, applicants may still choose to submit these scores if they wish, as the program accepts them [2]. Note that the GRE/GMAT requirement applies specifically to the joint MBA/MS program, not the standalone MS in Applied Data Science [3].
reference: No, the GRE/GMAT is not required for admission to the MS in Applied Data Science program. Applicants who have taken the test may still submit their scores if they wish — the GRE school code is 1832 and the GMAT school code is H9X-WG-70.

- Applicants who are currently studying in the United States on an F-1 visa and will transfer their SEVIS record from another U.S. institution may still apply by the remaining application deadlines', 'The MS in Applied Data Science program requires two letters of recommendation. We strongly recommend that at least one letter is written by someone such as a direct manager/supervisor or internship supervisor who can attest to skills you demonstrated or gained though a professional workplace experience (e.g., leadership, teamwork, collaboration, initiative, management, other).', 'Note: Letters of recommendation can be uploaded after you submit your application. Recommended sources for letters of recommendation include: professors; supervisors or managers; professional mentors.', 'We do not accept letters of recommendation from family members, friends, or peers.', 'The candidate statement is a key part of your application. The admissions committee pays careful attention to how you understand and present your aims and qualifications. The application will include a prompt with detailed instructions on the information the program is looking for in your candidate statement.', 'The Master’s in Applied Data Science admissions committee will review your resume. We encourage you to take the time to carefully update the resume you submit to us.', '- Work experience, including internships and part-time positions in the analytics/data science space
- Major accomplishments and awards
- Educational background, including areas of study
- Professional conferences attended', '- Undergraduate or graduate GPA', 'Your application contains a section on programming skills. Although applicants with limited skills in R or Python can and do get accepted into the program, the admissions committee values prior programming experience. Include a PDF of no more than two pages demonstrating current programming language skills.', 'Applicants to the Master’s in Applied Data Science program who do not meet the English Language Proficiency criteria must submit proof of English language proficiency.', 'When applying to the program, only your unofficial transcripts are required. If you are admitted into the program, then official transcripts must be sent to the following (note a bachelor’s degree is required to apply to the program):', 'If your institution cannot send your documents electronically, please have them send your transcripts to the following mailing address.', 'Please note, if documents are being mailed, we must receive them in their original, school-sealed envelope. If your documents are not received in a school-sealed envelope, they will be considered unofficial. Additionally, if your transcript does not list degree conferral information (degree earned and date of conferral), we will also need to receive an attested copy of your degree certificate.', 'Attention: MS in Applied Data Science Admissions', '455 N Cityfront Plaza Dr., Suite 2800']
response:
To apply for the MS in Applied Data Science at UChicago, you must submit the following materials:  

1. **Candidate Statement**: A key component of your application, where you detail your academic and professional goals, as well as your understanding of data science. The prompt provides specific instructions for this section [7].  
2. **Resume/CV**: Highlight work experience (including internships or part-time roles in analytics/data science), major accomplishments, educational background, professional conferences attended, and undergraduate/graduate GPA [8][9][10].  
3. **Letters of Recommendation**: Two letters are required. At least one should come from a professional supervisor or manager who can attest to your workplace skills (e.g., leadership, collaboration). Letters from family members, friends, or peers are not accepted [4][5][6].  
4. **Programming Supplement**: Submit a PDF (no more than two pages) demonstrating current programming skills in languages like R or Python. This includes a virtual portfolio if applicable [11].  
5. **Transcripts**: Unofficial transcripts are required initially. If admitted, official transcripts must be sent to the admissions office via mail or electronic submission. If mailed, they must be in a school-sealed envelope; otherwise, they will be considered unofficial [13][14][15][16].  
6. **English Language Proficiency**: Non-native English speakers must provide proof of proficiency, such as TOEFL or IELTS scores, unless exempt [12].  

Additional requirements include application fees, deadlines, and international student guidelines, which are detailed in the application portal [1][2][3].
reference: Applicants must submit two letters of recommendation, a candidate statement of no more than 700 words, a resume/CV, a programming supplement (a PDF of no more than two pages demonstrating R or Python skills), and a short virtual portfolio video (one required prompt, ~1:30 minutes). They also upload unofficial transcripts from each university attended and pay a $90 non-refundable application fee. Applicants who do not meet the English Language Proficiency criteria must additionally submit TOEFL or IELTS scores.

user_input: I'm an international student who needs visa sponsorship — which version of the MS in Applied Data Science can I apply to, and is it STEM/OPT eligible?
retrieved_contexts: ['- Only the full-time, in-person program is visa-eligible.\n- International students are also welcome to apply to our Master’s in Applied Data Science Online program, which does not require citizenship or permanent resident status and does not provide visa sponsorship.', 'Yes, the full-time, In-Person Master’s in Applied Data Science program is listed as a STEM-designated degree by the U.S. Department of Homeland Security for the purposes of the STEM OPT extension, allowing eligible students to apply. However, approval of STEM OPT is at the discretion of U.S. Citizenship & Immigration Services.', 'The In-Person program admits full- and part-time students for entrance in Autumn quarter annually. The full-time, in-person MS in Applied Data Science program is STEM/OPT eligible . Please visit the Online program page if you are interested in those full- and part-time options.']
response: As an international student requiring visa sponsorship, you should apply to the **full-time, in-person MS in Applied Data Science program**, as this is the only version offering visa sponsorship [1]. This program is also **STEM/OPT eligible**, as it is designated as a STEM degree by the U.S. Department of Homeland Security, allowing eligible students to apply for the STEM OPT extension. However, approval of STEM OPT is at the discretion of U.S. Citizenship & Immigration Services [2]. The online program does not provide visa sponsorship and is therefore not suitable for your needs [1].
reference: Only the full-time, in-person MS in Applied Data Science program is visa-eligible — both the 1-Year 12-Course pathway and the 2-Year Thesis Track 18-Course pathway qualify. The Online program does not provide visa sponsorship and does not require U.S. citizenship or permanent residency. The full-time, in-person program is also designated as a STEM degree by the U.S. Department of Homeland Security, so eligible students may apply for the STEM OPT extension (final approval is at USCIS discretion).

user_input: How many courses are in the 1-year MS in Applied Data Science track?
retrieved_contexts: ['Beginning in academic 2026-27, a limited number of In-Person, Full-Time students will have the opportunity to complete a 2-year version of the MS in Applied Data Science program. The 2-year program is completed over 21 months (2 academic years). Students in the 2-year program will complete 18 instead of 12 courses. The additional 6 courses consist of 4 additional elective courses (100 units each); and 2 required thesis courses (100 units each) that culminate in the completion of a required written thesis or thesis project. The thesis will be an extension of students’ previously completed Capstone Project. 2-year program students are highly encouraged to complete a research Capstone (as compared to a traditional, industry Capstone) for various reasons.', '- The MS in Data Science is a 10-course program based on UChicago’s Hyde Park campus. It includes a comprehensive research project and is designed for students who want to dive deeper into the theoretical and research side of data science—often as preparation for PhD study or research-focused careers.\n- The MS in Applied Data Science program offers both a 12-course track and an 18-course thesis track. It is offered in online and in-person formats, with in-person classes held at the NBC Tower in downtown Chicago. The program focuses on applying data science and machine learning methods to real-world problems through hands-on coursework, industry collaborations, and a two-quarter capstone or research project.']
response: The 1-year MS in Applied Data Science track consists of 12 courses. This is explicitly stated in the comparison between the 1-year program and the 2-year thesis track, which specifies the 1-year program includes 12 courses versus the 2-year program's 18 courses [1].
reference: The 1-year MS in Applied Data Science program is a 12-course track. The 12 courses break down into 6 Core courses, 4 Electives, and 2 Capstone courses. Students also complete a required Career Seminar throughout the program.

user_input: What's the difference between the 1-year 12-course program and the 2-year thesis track?
retrieved_contexts:
['The longer degree timeline offers more time to engage in academic and professional development while maintaining the same rigorous core curriculum and access to UChicago’s faculty and data science network. The first 12 of 18 courses that students complete in the 2-year program follow exactly the same course progression as those in the 1-year, 12-course program.', 'The signature 1-year 12 course program is ideal for those who wish to fast-track their graduate studies. Full-time students typically graduate in 4–5 quarters; part-time students may take up to 6 quarters. All students must graduate within 4 years maximum.', 'Courses are primarily offered in the evenings and on weekends to support working professionals. The program is STEM/OPT eligible .', 'All students complete:
1. 12 courses (6 Core, 4 Electives, 2 Capstone).
2. A required Career Seminar throughout the program. Those with 3+ years of full-time work experience may petition for exemption.
3. A 2-quarter Capstone Project with a real industry partner; some students opt to complete a research Capstone.', 'The 2-year program is not currently available to those in the Online program and/or those pursuing the MS in Applied Data Science on a part-time basis.', 'Beginning in academic 2026-27, a limited number of In-Person, Full-Time students will have the opportunity to complete a 2-year version of the MS in Applied Data Science program. The 2-year program is completed over 21 months (2 academic years). Students in the 2-year program will complete 18 instead of 12 courses. The additional 6 courses consist of 4 additional elective courses (100 units each); and 2 required thesis courses (100 units each) that culminate in the completion of a required written thesis or thesis project. The thesis will be an extension of students’ previously completed Capstone Project. 2-year program students are highly encouraged to complete a research Capstone (as compared to a traditional, industry Capstone) for various reasons.', 'Those admitted to the full-time, in-person thesis track pathway (18 courses, 6 quarters of coursework) are required to complete and submit a written thesis. The thesis will be an individually authored text based on an area of interest from students’ Research Capstone Project. The thesis will be managed through the required thesis courses (2, 100 units each). A lead faculty member with knowledge of the student’s area of interest will supervise the thesis project and provide resources and support as needed.', 'The 2-year Thesis Track (18 courses) pathway is optimized for those who want a longer master’s program, are interested in taking more elective courses, and have the capacity to complete a master’s thesis. The 2-year (21 month) pathway is completed over 6 academic quarters. The summer in between years 1 and 2 serves as a vacation quarter.', 'As an alternative to a traditional written thesis, students may opt to complete a thesis project that results in a ‘minimum viable product’ project. Through this option, students will complete a rigorous, multi-step ‘build’ and ‘marketing’ phase throughout the required 2 thesis courses. The thesis project will also be supervised by a program faculty member.']
response:
The 1-year 12-course program and the 2-year thesis track differ primarily in duration, course structure, and requirements. The 1-year program is designed for students seeking to fast-track their studies, requiring 12 courses (6 core, 4 electives, 2 capstone) completed in 4–5 quarters for full-time students or up to 6 quarters part-time [2]. It emphasizes industry-focused capstone projects and is STEM/OPT eligible [3]. 

The 2-year thesis track, available only to full-time in-person students starting in 2026–27, requires 18 courses (12 core + 6 additional electives and thesis courses) over 21 months [6]. Students complete a research-oriented capstone project, which serves as the foundation for a written thesis or thesis project (e.g., a minimum viable product) supervised by a faculty member [7][9]. This track emphasizes academic depth and original research, with a summer break between years 1 and 2 [8]. 

Both programs share the same core curriculum, but the 2-year track includes more electives and a mandatory thesis component, while the 1-year program focuses on practical, industry-aligned outcomes [1][6]. The 2-year track is not available to part-time or online students [5].
reference: The 1-year 12-course program requires 12 courses (6 Core, 4 Electives, 2 Capstone) and is available in both Full- and Part-Time formats; full-time students typically graduate in 4-5 quarters and part-time students may take up to 6 quarters. The 2-Year Thesis Track is Full-Time only, completed over 6 academic quarters (about 21 months), and requires 18 total courses (6 Core, 8 Electives, 2 Capstone, 2 Independent Study) plus a Master's Thesis, which can be a traditional written thesis or a project-based thesis. The 2-year track is intended for students who want a longer program with more electives and the capacity to complete a thesis.

user_input: What does my company need to provide if we sponsor a capstone project?
retrieved_contexts:

- Two or more points of contact
- Access to data relevant to the project by the first week of the applicable quarter

response:
If your company sponsors a capstone project, you will need to provide the following:  
- A detailed problem statement with a description of the data and expected results [1].  
- Two or more points of contact [1].  
- Access to relevant data by the first week of the applicable quarter [1].  
- Engagement through regular meetings (typically bi-weekly) while classes are in session [1].  
- If requested, a non-disclosure agreement that may be completed by the student team [1].  

There is no monetary cost or contract required for sponsorship [1].
reference: Sponsors are asked to provide a detailed problem statement describing the data and expected results, two or more points of contact, access to the relevant data by the first week of the applicable quarter, and engagement through regular (typically bi-weekly) meetings while classes are in session. If requested, sponsors can also have the student team sign a non-disclosure agreement.

user_input: Can you give me some examples of past capstone projects students have worked on?
retrieved_contexts: ['UChicago Applied Data Science students designed an AI-powered app that transforms children’s prompts into personalized stories. Using natural language processing and machine learning, the team built a system that generates dynamic, age-appropriate narratives in real time, showcasing how data science can merge creativity with technology to bring imagination to life.', 'As part of their Capstone Project, a team of MS in Applied Data Science students developed SkySearch, a drone-based vision system that enables drones to identify and locate objects in real-world environments using simple human prompts.', 'From predicting floods to powering potential clinical breakthroughs, students in the University of Chicago MS in Applied Data Science program are using their skills to make a real impact.']
response: Students in the UChicago MS in Applied Data Science program have worked on diverse capstone projects, such as an AI-powered storytelling app that uses natural language processing and machine learning to generate personalized, age-appropriate narratives in real time [1], and SkySearch, a drone-based vision system enabling drones to identify and locate objects based on simple human prompts [2]. Another example highlights projects focused on "Data Science for Good," which include applications like flood prediction and clinical breakthroughs, though specific project names are not detailed in this context [3].
reference: Past capstone projects in the archive include Argonne's Rezzy AI support chatbot for an EV charger reservation app, Inference Analytics' automated ICD-10 code prediction using LLMs, an MCP-driven multi-agent RAG system for prostate cancer summarization, Aetna's optimization engine for scenario planning, Chicago Baseball's pitcher motion-tracking project, and HERE Technologies' vehicle perception data alignment work. Other archived sponsors include Global IQ, LatentView Analytics, and Ateema.

[node_id: section:0288fccdc1108361] [type: Section]', 'The culminating experience in the Master’s in Applied Data Science program is a Capstone Project . You will immerse yourself in a real business problem and will gain valuable, data driven insights using authentic data. Together with project sponsors, you will develop a data science solution to address organization problems, enhance analytics capabilities, and expand talent pools and employment opportunities. Leveraging the university’s rich research portfolio, you also have the option to join a research-focused team . Hear directly from a faculty advisor in this video about how students engage in research-driven capstone projects with real-world applications.']
response:
The capstone project in the UChicago MS in Applied Data Science program is a two-quarter requirement covering research design, implementation, and writing [1][2][3][4][5][6][7]. Full-time students typically begin in their third quarter, while part-time students start in their fifth quarter [3][4][5][6][7].  

Regarding project focus, the program allows students to engage in **research-focused capstone projects** as an option, particularly through partnerships with faculty and leveraging the university’s research portfolio [8][9]. While the evidence does not explicitly state whether students must choose between industry and research projects, it highlights that research-driven initiatives are available and emphasize real-world applications [8][9]. Students interested in research-focused work can explore opportunities through the program’s collaboration with faculty and research partners.
reference: The capstone is the culminating experience of the MS-ADS program, where students work on a real business problem with project sponsors using authentic data to develop a data science solution. Students also have the option to join a research-focused team that leverages the university's research portfolio, with a faculty advisor, instead of an industry-sponsored project. Featured industry-sponsored examples include the AI-Powered Storytelling App and SkySearch drone vision system, while the broader archive lists projects with sponsors like Argonne, Aetna, and HERE Technologies.

user_input: How much does the MS in Applied Data Science program cost per course?
retrieved_contexts: ['- Tuition for the 12- and 18-course MS in Applied Data Science program: $6,384 per course*/$76,608+^, 12 courses/$114,912+^, 18 courses\n- Program Enrollment Deposit (non-refundable): $1,500 (credited toward your first quarter’s tuition balance)']
response: The MS in Applied Data Science program costs **$6,384 per course** [1]. This rate applies to both the 12-course and 18-course tracks, with total program costs listed as $76,608+ for 12 courses or $114,912+ for 18 courses [1].
reference: Tuition is $6,384 per course, which works out to $76,608 for the 12-course program or $114,912 for the 18-course program. There is also a non-refundable $1,500 program enrollment deposit that is credited toward the first quarter's tuition balance.

response: The Online MS in Applied Data Science program is more flexible for full-time workers compared to the In-Person program. The Online program is explicitly designed for working professionals, offering live classes that align with their schedules and providing remote access to coursework [1][2]. It also emphasizes support for full-time employees, including dedicated resources for online learning and instructional design expertise [4]. In contrast, while the In-Person program offers evening and weekend classes to accommodate working professionals [7], there is no explicit mention of additional flexibility beyond fixed class times. The Online program’s structure inherently allows greater scheduling flexibility for those balancing full-time work [2][6].
reference: The Online program is generally more flexible for full-time workers — it combines weekly synchronous classes (weekday evenings at 6 pm CT and occasional Saturdays at 9 am CT) with asynchronous content you complete on your own schedule, and is optimized for students with 3+ years of full-time work experience. The In-Person program also offers part-time options, with courses primarily 6–9 pm Monday through Thursday at NBC Tower or Gleacher Center in downtown Chicago, but requires you to attend classes in person and is geared more toward students earlier in their careers (1+ year of work experience and relevant internships).

user_input: How does the UChicago MS in Applied Data Science compare to Northwestern's MS in Data Science program?

It is offered in online and in-person formats, with in-person classes held at the NBC Tower in downtown Chicago. The program focuses on applying data science and machine learning methods to real-world problems through hands-on coursework, industry collaborations, and a two-quarter capstone or research project.', 'Take the next step to advance your career with UChicago’s MS in Applied Data Science.', 'The two programs share a strong foundation in data science but differ in structure, location, and focus.', 'UChicago’s Master’s in Applied Data Science program recruits industry leaders from across the country. - **UChicago’s MS in Data Science** is a 10-course program focused on theoretical foundations and research, often preparing students for PhD study or research careers [2].
